passage.text:
Campus Fun Run
Date: Saturday, June 12 | Time: 9:00 A.M.-12:00 P.M.
Location: Riverside Park
(two blocks south of Willis Stadium)
Event Highlights
5K and 10K races
Kids' 1-kilometer fun run
Live music performances
Food trucks and refreshment stands
Health information booths
Registration Fees
5K race: $30
10K race: $40 - Kids' fun run: $10
Proceeds: All registration fees will benefit the Joy Byers Children's Hospital, helping provide healthcare and support
to children in need.
What to Bring:
Comfortable running shoes
Water bottle
Sunscreen
Volunteer Information: We need volunteers to help with race setup, water stations, and clean-up.
Contact Information: To register or volunteer, visit www.riversidefunrun.com or drop by the Riverside Community
Note: Early registration ends on June 1. On-site registration will be available on the day of the event. Join us for a
morning of fitness, fun, and community spirit!

passage.text:
-•
Post by Laura Kim, Fitness Coach
Hey, everyone! I just wrapped up an incredible fitness boot camp in the
beautiful city of Sydney, Australia. I wanted to share some highlights and
tips from the camp with you all.
• Day 1: We started with an intense cardio session at Bondi Beach.
The fresh ocean air and stunning views made the workout feel energizing
and refreshing.
• Day 2: Focused on strength training at a local gym. We used a variety
of equipment, including dumbbells, resistance bands, and kettlebells.
• Day 3: Took a break from intense workouts and did a yoga session at
a park overlooking the Sydney Opera House. The serene environment
helped everyone relax and recharge.
• Day 4: Ended the camp with a group hike through the Blue Mountains.
It was challenging but absolutely worth it for the breathtaking scenery.
Throughout the boot camp, we emphasized the importance of proper
nutrition and hydration. I shared tips on how to maintain a balanced diet
and stay hydrated, especially during intense physical activities. I'm already
looking forward to the next boot camp! If you have any questions about the
workouts or want more fitness tips, feel free to ask in the comments below!
#FitnessBootCamp #Sydney #HealthyLiving #Workout
• Comment
® Like

passage.text:
Post by Laura Kim, Fitness Coach
Hey, everyone! I just wrapped up an incredible fitness boot camp in the
beautiful city of Sydney, Australia. I wanted to share some highlights and
tips from the camp with you all.
• Day 1: We started with an intense cardio session at Bondi Beach.
The fresh ocean air and stunning views made the workout feel energizing
and refreshing.
• Day 2: Focused on strength training at a local gym. We used a variety
of equipment, including dumbbells, resistance bands, and kettlebells.
• Day 3: Took a break from intense workouts and did a yoga session at
a park overlooking the Sydney Opera House. The serene environment
helped everyone relax and recharge.
• Day 4: Ended the camp with a group hike through the Blue Mountains.
It was challenging but absolutely worth it for the breathtaking scenery.
Throughout the boot camp, we emphasized the importance of proper
nutrition and hydration. I shared tips on how to maintain a balanced diet
and stay hydrated, especially during intense physical activities. I'm already
looking forward to the next boot camp! If you have any questions about the
workouts or want more fitness tips, feel free to ask in the comments below!
#FitnessBootCamp #Sydney #HealthyLiving #Workout

questions.directive: Read the passage and choose the best answer.
questions.prompt: All of the following are mentioned as part of the formation process of opals EXCEPT:

passage.text:
Opal is a unique gemstone, known for its vibrant colors that shift as the stone moves. Unlike most gemstones, which form rigid, repeating crystalline structures, opals are

amorphous, meaning they lack a fixed internal shape. This is due to the formation process, where silica-rich water seeps into cracks in rocks deep underground, leaving silica deposits

when it evaporates. Tiny silica spheres create the internal structure of opals, and these

spheres diffract light, separating it into colors, much like a prism does.

or and brilliance of opals depends on the size and spacing of the silica spheres.

Larger spheres produce reds and oranges, while smaller spheres result in blues and

greens. Because of Australia's unique geological conditions, it is home to over 90 percent of the world's opals. Opals are also fragile. Their high water content and noncrystalline

structure make them prone to cracking when exposed to sudden temperature changes. Modern jewelers use techniques such as laser cutting and humidity-controlled storage to

better protect opals.

Ancient cultures believed opals had mystical properties and brought good luck. The

Romans saw them as a symbol of hope and purity, while the Greeks thought they gave the gift of prophecy.

202611209

Unlike most gemstones, which form rigid, repeating crystalline structures, opals are amorphous, meaning they lack a fixed internal shape. This is due to the formation process, where silica-rich water seeps into cracks in rocks deep underground, leaving silica deposits when it evaporates. Tiny silica spheres create the internal structure of opals, and these spheres diffract light, separating it into colors, much like a prism does. The color and brilliance of opals depends on the size and spacing of the silica spheres. Larger spheres produce reds and oranges, while smaller spheres result in blues and greens. Because of Australia's unique geological conditions, it is home to over 90 percent of the world's opals. Opals are also fragile. Their high water content and noncrystalline structure make them prone to cracking when exposed to sudden temperature changes. Modern jewelers use techniques such as laser cutting and humidity-controlled storage to better protect opals. Ancient cultures believed opals had mystical properties and brought good luck. The Romans saw them as a symbol of hope and purity, while the Greeks thought they gave the gift of prophecy.

questions.directive: Read the passage and choose the best answer.
questions.prompt: What is the passage mainly about?

passage.text:
Innovative playwriting techniques have transformed the theatrical landscape by challenging linear

storytelling and passive spectatorship. Nonlinear narratives, seen in plays like Harold Pinter's Betrayal, disrupt chronological order to reflect the fragmented nature of memory and emotion. This

structure invites audiences to actively reconstruct the story, deepening their engagement.

Another innovation is the so-called breaking of the fourth wall, where characters directly address the audience. Used by Bertolt Brecht in Mother Courage and Her Children, this technique encourages viewers to reflect critically rather than become absorbed in illusion. Fully interactive theater,

exemplified by the British production Sleep No More, goes further by allowing viewers to physically

move through the performance space, personalizing the experience and making each viewing unique.

These techniques suggest a broader hypothesis: that theatrical form is not merely a container for content but a vital part of creating meaning. By reshaping narrative structure and audience dynamics, playwrights can evoke new emotional and intellectual responses, revealing complex aspects of the

human condition.

Of course, such innovations may confuse rather than enlighten more traditional-minded theater

audiences and also disrupt their emotional immersion, and fragment the narrative. Thus, while these techniques can serve as powerful tools, their success depends on thoughtful execution and a clear

artistic purpose.

202641409

Home Module2 - Upper

options.text: The influence of traditional theater on modern audience
The role of audience participation in theatrical performances
New approaches to theatrical storytelling and audience involvement
The challenges of interpreting nonlinear narratives in drama Next > Review A < Back
